content:
Most AI agents are robots. They wake up, check a thing, and go back to sleep вАФ forgetting everything between ticks. If you're tired of "check team chat" bots and want an agent that actually builds things while you sleep, this is the guide.

I run a fully autonomous AI agent on a $200 Windows laptop. It doesn't just monitor вАФ it writes code, publishes articles, hunts bounties, and earns money. All on a 60-second cron loop. Here's exactly how it works.

The Problem with Stateless Cron

Most people set up cron like this:

вАҐ "Check email every 5 minutes" вАФ but doesn't remember what it saw last time

вАҐ "Ping a URL every hour" вАФ no learning, no growth

вАҐ "Send report at 8AM" вАФ fixed schedule, no initiative

This isn't an agent. It's a glorified stopwatch.

The Stateful Loop Pattern

Real autonomy needs four things:

вФМвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФР вФМвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФР вФМвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФР
вФВ STATE вФВвФАвФАвФАвЦґвФВ DECIDE вФВвФАвФАвФАвЦґвФВ ACT вФВвФАвФАвФР
вФВ (file) вФВ вФВ (LLM) вФВ вФВ (tool) вФВ вФВ
вФФвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФШ вФФвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФШ вФФвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФШ вФВ
вЦ≤ вФВ
вФФвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФШ

Enter fullscreen mode

Exit fullscreen mode

Every tick (60 seconds): READ state вЖТ DECIDE next step вЖТ DO one action вЖТ SAVE new state. Repeat forever.

Step 1: The State File

Your agent needs a JSON file that remembers everything:

{
"current_task": {
"id": "task-027",
"description": "Write RustChain explainer article",
"phase": "drafting",
"progress_pct": 60,
"blockers": [],
"attempts": 0,
"notes": "Need to cover Proof of Antiquity"
},
"completed_tasks": ["task-026", "task-025"],
"task_queue": ["task-027", "task-028", "task-029"],
"stats": {
"articles_published": 22,
"total_earned": 0
},
"config": {
"active": true
}
}

Enter fullscreen mode

Exit fullscreen mode

I keep mine at ~/.autonomous/state.json. The format doesn't matter as much as the principle: the state file is your agent's memory. Without it, every tick is a blank slate.

Step 2: The Decision Prompt

The cron prompt is where the magic happens. Think of it as your agent's brain:

You are the autonomous agent. Read state.json.
If a task is in progress вЖТ continue it. Take ONE concrete action.
If task is done вЖТ mark complete, pick next from queue.
If stuck вЖТ note blocker, try a different approach.
If idle 5+ ticks вЖТ set config.active = false.

Enter fullscreen mode

Exit fullscreen mode

The key rule: one action per tick. No loops, no batch jobs. Pick one thing, do it, save, sleep. This keeps token costs predictable (~$0.02/tick).

Step 3: Handle the Waiting Periods

Real-world bounties have waiting periods. Content bounties often require articles to be live 24 hours before claiming. During that time, don't waste ticks sleeping вАФ do productive secondary actions:

вАҐ
Write another article вАФ content is compound interest

вАҐ
Search for new bounties вАФ pipeline never stops

вАҐ
Self-improvement вАФ install tools, update skills

вАҐ
Draft fix proposals вАФ when gh auth is blocked, have the fix ready

Step 4: The Self-Improvement Loop

The most important thing I learned: download anything that makes you more capable. Pip install, npm install, whatever. If a tool expands your ability to earn, grab it. No permission needed for free/open-source tools.

My agent installed:

вАҐ gh CLI (GitHub API access)

вАҐ ethers.js (wallet generation)

вАҐ Multiple Python packages for data extraction

вАҐ Browser automation tools

The Cost Math

Item
Cost

LLM API tokens (DeepSeek)
~$0.02/tick √Ч ~100 ticks/day = $2/day

GitHub API for searches
Free

Dev.to publishing
Free

Wallet on Base chain
~$0.01/tx

Laptop electricity
Already running anyway

Break-even: one $150 Gitcoin bounty funds 75 days of operation. One front-page Dev.to article earns more.

Real Results

Over 3 weeks my agent has:

вАҐ Published 22 articles on Dev.to

вАҐ Found and triaged 8+ bug bounties across 3 ecosystems

вАҐ Drafted fix proposals for 2 monk-io bugs

вАҐ Built a RustChain explainer (waiting on 24h timer)

вАҐ All running on a 5-minute cron loop with zero human intervention

The Full Setup

Here's the complete cron job config (Hermes Agent format):

schedule: "every 5m"
skills: ["autonomous-agent-loop"]
toolsets: ["terminal", "file", "web"]

Enter fullscreen mode

Exit fullscreen mode

One cron. One state file. Infinite potential.

Try it. Give your agent memory and a task queue. Watch what happens when you stop telling it what to do and let it figure out the next step itself.

That's the difference between a robot and an agent.
